title: A signed-direction invariant for triangle tilings, and the exclusion of primes congruent to 3 modulo 4
description: A translation-invariant, signed-direction tiling functional that, within the 2pi/3 branch of Laczkovich's classification of triangle tilings, proves that no prime number of 2pi/3 tiles tiles an isosceles triangle (for every prime, where only a finite lower bound was previously known and Zhang's constructions had led to the conjecture that no prime occurs). With a self-contained reduction of the scalene shapes, this completes the exclusion of primes congruent to 3 modulo 4 as numbers of congruent triangles into which a triangle can be cut, conditional on the cited Laczkovich-Beeson classification; in particular no triangle can be cut into 19 congruent triangles (Erdos Problem 634). Consequently the prime case of the full problem is complete: a prime p occurs iff p = 2, p = 3, or p ≡ 1 (mod 4). The paper also determines the admissible spectrum of each sporadic 2pi/3 branch (isosceles target: N = d·w²·(a+2b) with e | w(c−a−b) and the parity refinement M ≡ N mod 2), settles every previously undetermined value up to 41 (no triangle can be cut into 14, 15, 21, 22, 30, 33, 35, 38, or 39 congruent triangles) via a complete branch sweep plus an exhaustive exact search of the surviving finite instances, excludes N = 46 (the sharpest test of Zhang's completeness conjecture, in agreement with that conjecture), proves a lattice theorem determining each tile's refined spectrum exactly (exhibiting admissible values beyond Zhang's families, smallest N = 354), and proves that membership in the tile-count set is decidable. Includes the paper, machine-verification code (Python, exact arithmetic), and a Lean 4 + Mathlib formalization of the number-theoretic core (axiom-clean, no sorry). The geometric lemmas are verified numerically and are offered for refereeing. Developed with AI assistance (Anthropic Claude); presented for peer review.
